title: Living in Boulder blog 7 - 27 - 2017: Justinian Law Code, Roman buildings book fm P &Ash

enrichment.summary: Kyle gives a late-night blog update from Boulder, discussing his work cataloging ancient books (including the Justinian Law Code and a Platner & Ashby Roman buildings dictionary) for an education page on his website. He also mentions visiting his mom at a hospital, eating food she provided, and plans to learn Adobe Creative Cloud programs.
enrichment.speaker: kyle

stances.position: Finds it significant and large (~960 pages); worth cataloging on his education page

stances.topic: Platner and Ashby Roman buildings dictionary
stances.position: Prefers it over Wikipedia's list because it covers ~32 buildings vs Wikipedia's 12; wants to include it on his site
Has had it for a couple years but hasn't learned the programs; wants to watch all the tutorials
Excited to finish and organize all books by page count; notes some authors have far more pages than others
